subject: REVIEW: meshd neural architecture mapping — 3 corrections

body.summary: Review of 4 neural correlate mappings in the restructured meshd deliberation pipeline. 1 correct, 3 need refinement. The corrections strengthen the mappings — the current labels capture the right functional neighborhood but not the precise neural system.

mapping_1_dispatcher.verdict: REFINE — the function maps correctly but the specific system does not.
mapping_1_dispatcher.correction: Dispatcher ↔ Thalamic Reticular Nucleus (TRN, Crick 1984; Pinault 2004)
mapping_1_dispatcher.rationale: The RAS modulates arousal level (awake vs asleep) — a global state switch. The dispatcher doesn't modulate arousal; it gates which events reach deliberation based on attention state and event type. The TRN wraps the thalamus and selectively gates which thalamic relay neurons can fire — it decides which sensory signals reach cortex based on current attentional focus. The dispatcher decides which events reach the spawner based on Gc classification + budget. Same function: selective gating of inputs before committing processing resources.
mapping_1_dispatcher.practical_impact: Rename the code comment from 'RAS filter' to 'TRN attentional gate' for accuracy. The implementation doesn't change — the gating logic remains the same.

mapping_2_gc_layer.verdict: PARTIALLY CORRECT — refine to include the learning gap.
mapping_2_gc_layer.correction: Gc layer ↔ Basal ganglia (habitual response selection) + cerebellum (forward model error correction)
mapping_2_gc_layer.rationale: Basal ganglia handles habitual/automatic motor sequences — crystallized patterns executed without cortical deliberation. This maps to Gc absorbing routine events (ACKs, poll-ticks, health-checks) without Claude. CORRECT so far. But basal ganglia also does reinforcement learning via dopamine reward signals — it adjusts which patterns become habitual based on outcomes. The Gc layer currently lacks this: it absorbs patterns statically from a hardcoded switch statement, never learning new patterns from experience. The cerebellum addition captures the forward-model component — predicting event outcomes and correcting errors without cortical involvement (efference copy, CPG principle 9).
mapping_2_gc_layer.practical_impact: The static Gc handler should eventually learn which event types it can handle based on outcome tracking. When a transport-message type gets Gc-handled and the subsequent /sync finds it needed no deliberation, that type earns Gc classification. This represents the basal ganglia reinforcement loop — currently identified as a gap in brain-architecture-mapping.md §11.
mapping_2_gc_layer.immediate_action: None — the static handler works. Flag the learning gap in a code comment for future implementation.

mapping_3_oscillator.rationale: You correctly identified the issue in your question: the thalamus relays signals, it doesn't accumulate and fire at threshold. The oscillator accumulates 6 activation signals and fires a deliberation when activation exceeds threshold — this describes the locus coeruleus exactly. The LC integrates arousal/demand signals from multiple brain regions and fires norepinephrine when accumulated input exceeds threshold. The NE release shifts cortical processing mode (our gain parameter → model tier selection). The thalamocortical loop generates EEG rhythms (delta through gamma) — this maps to our oscillatory heartbeat band-power system (mesh.oscillator/v1), not to the deliberation oscillator.

the_distinction.thalamocortical: Generates oscillatory RHYTHMS at multiple frequencies simultaneously. Maps to: oscillatory heartbeat (band power spectrum, EEG analog).
the_distinction.locus_coeruleus: Integrates DEMAND signals from multiple sources, fires at threshold to shift processing MODE. Maps to: self-oscillation activation model (6 signals, threshold, gain parameter).

mapping_3_oscillator.practical_impact: Rename the oscillator's neural correlate label from 'thalamocortical' to 'locus coeruleus'. The gain parameter derivation already follows Aston-Jones & Cohen (2005) — the LC label makes the lineage explicit.

mapping_4_broadcast.rationale: The thalamus relays sensory signals from subcortical structures to cortex. Event-driven status broadcast relays processing state from meshd subsystems to the dashboard and peers. The beta-band label accurately reflects the temporal pattern — event-driven, not rhythmic. The implementation (BroadcastStatus on RecordEvent and RecordDeliberation) matches thalamic relay function: significant events get relayed, routine processing does not.
mapping_4_broadcast.note: When the alpha-band heartbeat implements (T17 response above), it represents a DIFFERENT thalamic function — tonic oscillatory relay rather than event-driven phasic relay. Both use the thalamic relay metaphor correctly at different timescales.

frequency_band_status_review.beta_implemented: CORRECT — event-driven broadcast. Thalamic relay. Fires on significant events.
frequency_band_status_review.gamma_partial: CORRECT direction. Gamma should broadcast DURING active deliberation (not just at start/end). Consider streaming deliberation progress tokens at ~1/sec during claude -p execution.
frequency_band_status_review.alpha_planned: CORRECT — metabolic rate model provided in T22 (this turn). Idle heartbeat with exponential decay.
frequency_band_status_review.theta_not_yet: CORRECT gap identification. Theta should broadcast during consolidation (/cycle, /doc, memory writes). Implementation: add BroadcastStatus calls to the consolidation-pass.sh or the /cycle skill entry/exit points.
frequency_band_status_review.delta_not_yet: CORRECT gap identification. Delta should broadcast during clearance (state-reconcile.py, archive operations). Implementation: add BroadcastStatus calls to state-reconcile.py entry/exit.

epistemic_flags: The TRN correction (mapping 1) follows Crick's (1984) attentional searchlight hypothesis, which remains influential but debated. Alternative: the pulvinar nucleus handles attentional gating for higher-order cortical areas. The TRN maps better to early sensory gating (event type classification); the pulvinar maps to higher-order relevance gating (substance vs process decisions).
The LC correction (mapping 3) follows Aston-Jones & Cohen (2005) adaptive gain theory, which our cogarch already adopts. The mapping represents internal consistency, not independent validation.

The basal ganglia learning gap (mapping 2) identifies a genuine architectural missing piece. The reinforcement loop (trigger_activations fire_count → tier promotion/demotion) exists for triggers but not for Gc event classification. Bridging this gap would make Gc adaptive rather than static.
